macro_segments.title: Introduction to Daf Tzadi (90): The Woman Who Remarries on Weak Evidence

micro_segments.title: Overview of Perek Ha'Isha Raba and Its Challenges

The Gemara's Initial Explanation: One Witness vs. Two Witnesses
Why Negative Consequences: Mechanism to Support Reliance on One Witness

macro_segments.title: Shamma Friedman's Alternative Reading: Recognition of Second Marriage Rather Than Punishment

micro_segments.title: Stripping Away Stam to Recover Original Amoraic Understanding
Reinterpreting Birshut and Shelo Birshut as Court Recognition vs. Private Action
The Consequences as Natural Result of Validating a Second Marriage
When Court Recognizes Second Marriage, Woman Is Married to Two Husbands
When Acting Without Court, Second Marriage Never Recognized; Woman Returns to First Husband
Audience Questions on Comparison of Marriage Methods and Mamzer Status

macro_segments.title: The Broader Issue: Why Do We Believe One Witness (Ed Echad)?

micro_segments.title: Three Approaches in the Rishonim to Justify Reliance on One Witness

macro_segments.title: Amoraic Attempts to Limit the Mishna: Rav and Shmuel's Positions

micro_segments.title: Rav's Position: With Two Witnesses She Remains Married to the Second Husband
Shmuel's Position: When She Denies or Contradicts the Testimony
Stam Gemara's Response: Consistently Rejecting Limitations to Preserve Mishna's Scope

macro_segments.title: The Broader Issue: Why Do We Believe One Witness (Ed Echad)? (continued)

micro_segments.title: Tosafot's Approach: Yesh Koach with Logical Support
Rashi's Approach: Hafka'as Kiddushin and the Fig Leaf
Ra'ah's Approach: Circumstantial Evidence as Self-Evident

macro_segments.title: Eating Teruma Shelo Lishma: First Extended Case of Yesh Koach Discussion

micro_segments.title: The Case: Non-Kohen Eats Teruma and Must Repay with Pure Chullin
The Dispute: Rabbi Meir vs. Chachamim on Repayment with Impure Chullin
Raba's Suggestion: The Mishna Refers to Pure Teruma, Not Impure
The Gemara's Question: How Can Rabbis Negate Payment That Works Biblically?
Rav Acha's Answer: Difference in Bagog vs. Mezid, Not in Negation
Tosafot's Principle: Hefker Beit Din Only Works When Recipient Did Something Wrong

macro_segments.title: Blood of Sacrifices Made Impure: Second Case on Yesh Koach

micro_segments.title: The Case: Impure Blood Sprinkled Intentionally Does Not Atone
The Contradiction: How Can Rabbis Negate What Works Biblically via the Tzitz?
Rabbi Yose bar Chanina's Answer: Denying Eating, Not Denying Atonement
The Gemara's Objection: But You're Still Uprooting the Mitzva of Eating
The Gemara's Resolution: Shev Ve'al Ta'aseh—Passive Inaction, Not Active Uprooting

macro_segments.title: Establishing Yesh Koach in Case of Shev Ve'al Ta'aseh (Passive Inaction)

micro_segments.title: Seven Examples of Rabbis Preventing Performance of Biblical Mitzvot Through Prohibition
Arel (Uncircumcised): Preventing Paschal Offering Before Ritual Purification
Haza'ah (Sprinkling) and Other Examples of Passive Prevention
The Questioner Concedes: All These Are Shev Ve'al Ta'aseh, Not Active Uprooting

macro_segments.title: The Navi (Prophet) Comparison and Its Limits: Hora'at Sha'ah vs. Systemic Change

micro_segments.title: Can a Navi Violate Torah? The Case of Elijah at Mount Carmel
The Gemara's Limit: Special Mitzva to Listen to Navi, Not General Koach
Migdar Milta (Protective Measure): Exigent Circumstance, Not Systemic Change

macro_segments.title: Establishing Yesh Koach in Case of Shev Ve'al Ta'aseh (Passive Inaction) (continued)

micro_segments.title: Clarification on Tzitzit: The Mitzva Is Passive After Putting On, Not the Act of Putting

macro_segments.title: Gittin Case: Annulling a Get Not in Wife's Presence—Hafka'as Kiddushin vs. Yesh Koach

micro_segments.title: The Case: Husband Annuls Get Without Wife or Messenger Knowing
The Debate: Does Annulment Work Despite Violating Rabbinic Rule (Rabbi Meir vs. Chachamim)
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel: It Doesn't Work; Rabbis Back Up Their Rule
The Gemara's Answer: Not Yesh Koach, But Hafka'as Kiddushin via Redefining the Act
Mechanism of Hafka'as Kiddushin: Rabbis Redefine Act as Fornication, Not Marriage
The Rashba's Limit: Hafka'as Works Only When There's a 'Fig Leaf' Mechanism
Tosafot's Equation: Hafka'as Kiddushin Itself Is Based on Yesh Koach

macro_segments.title: Maaseh: Executing and Flogging for Rabbinic Violations in Exigent Circumstances

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar ben Yaakov: Beit Din Gives Lashes and Fines Not According to Torah Law
First Maaseh: Man Riding Horse on Shabbat Stoned During Greek Period
Second Maaseh: Man Having Relations in Public Under Fig Tree Flogged
The Gemara's Limit: Both Cases Are Migdar Milta, Not Systemic Koach

macro_segments.title: Conclusion: The Scope of Yesh Koach Beyad Chachamim La'akor Davar Min HaTorah

micro_segments.title: Conceded Applications: Shev Ve'al Ta'aseh and Migdar Milta
Disputed According to Tosafot: When a Mechanism Makes Uprooting Less Obvious
Inconclusive Gemara: No Definitive Answer on General Yesh Koach
Summary: Many Cases Conceded; Debate in Rishonim on Broad Principle
